template.title: Gitlab Fronting Starter
template.summary: Template-suggested Gitlab API/MCP fronting structure with reviewed capability candidates.
template.description: Starts a governed Gitlab fronting project from reviewed starter data. Verify backend operations, auth posture, and policy boundaries against the real integration before locking.
template.recommendedBrief: Create a governed ANIP fronting layer for Gitlab. Expose selected behavior-shaped capabilities instead of raw backend operations. Read operations must preserve actor-visible scope. Write-adjacent operations must prepare previews or require approval before downstream mutation.

documents.title: Gitlab governed fronting intent
documents.content:
# Gitlab Governed Fronting Intent

> Imported from starter template `gitlab-fronting-starter`. Treat this as draft guidance, not discovered truth.

Expose a governed Gitlab capability surface in front of native API or MCP-backed operations.

## Template-suggested capabilities
- gitlab.project.search_context: Search bounded GitLab project issues and merge requests without exposing raw project tools.
- gitlab.issue.prepare: Prepare a GitLab issue preview with labels and assignees before any issue is created.
- gitlab.mr.comment.prepare: Prepare a merge request comment from review or release context without posting it.
- gitlab.pipeline.trigger.request: Prepare a governed GitLab pipeline trigger request and stop for approval.
- gitlab.release_notes.prepare: Prepare bounded release notes from selected issues or merge requests without creating a GitLab release.

## Governance posture
- Raw Gitlab tools are not exposed directly to agents.
- Reads return bounded actor-visible context.
- Write-adjacent actions prepare previews or require explicit approval before execution.
- Missing required inputs trigger clarification instead of guessing.

documents.title: Gitlab integration evidence
documents.content:
# Gitlab Integration Evidence Template

> Imported from starter template `gitlab-fronting-starter`. Verify every operation against your chosen native API/MCP source before locking.

Native API connection: GITLAB_API_URL
MCP connection: GITLAB_MCP_ENDPOINT

## Template-suggested backend operations
- gitlab.api.search_project_context: GET /gitlab/projects/{namespace}/{project}/context/search
- gitlab.api.prepare_issue: POST /gitlab/projects/{namespace}/{project}/issues/preview
- gitlab.api.prepare_merge_request_comment: POST /gitlab/projects/{namespace}/{project}/merge_requests/{merge_request_iid}/comments/preview
- gitlab.api.request_pipeline_trigger: POST /gitlab/projects/{namespace}/{project}/pipelines/{pipeline_ref}/trigger/preview
- gitlab.api.prepare_release_notes: GET /gitlab/projects/{namespace}/{project}/release-notes/preview

## Secret posture
- Studio stores secret references only; project exports must not contain tokens.
- Teams can implement either native API or MCP backend seams behind the same governed ANIP contract.

documents.title: Gitlab developer evidence
documents.content:
# Gitlab Developer Evidence

Imported from starter template `gitlab-fronting-starter`. This is developer-owned evidence for Studio Autopilot and generated-service review.

This document turns starter operations into explicit ANIP runtime governance and input-contract evidence. Review it against the real integration before publishing.

## Capability Evidence Summary

| capability_id | service_id | operation_type | side_effect_level | inputs | backend_operation |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | ---: | --- |
| gitlab.project.search_context | gitlab-fronting | read | read | 5 | gitlab.api.search_project_context |
| gitlab.issue.prepare | gitlab-fronting | write | write_adjacent | 7 | gitlab.api.prepare_issue |
| gitlab.mr.comment.prepare | gitlab-fronting | write | write_adjacent | 6 | gitlab.api.prepare_merge_request_comment |
| gitlab.pipeline.trigger.request | gitlab-governance | write | write_adjacent | 6 | gitlab.api.request_pipeline_trigger |
| gitlab.release_notes.prepare | gitlab-fronting | read | read | 5 | gitlab.api.prepare_release_notes |

## Reviewed Developer Evidence

The starter also carries first-class `integration_fronting_capability_mapping` records. Use this document as readable developer evidence, not as the only machine-readable source.

### Runtime governance

| capability_id | execution_posture | intent_type | produces | does_not_produce |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| gitlab.project.search_context | read_only | read_only | content.summary, data.read | raw_data_export, system.mutation |
| gitlab.issue.prepare | prepare_only | prepare_only | approval.request, system.preview_mutation, content.draft | approval.execute, system.mutation, raw_data_export |
| gitlab.mr.comment.prepare | prepare_only | prepare_only | approval.request, system.preview_mutation, content.draft | approval.execute, system.mutation, raw_data_export |
| gitlab.pipeline.trigger.request | prepare_only | prepare_only | approval.request, system.preview_mutation, content.draft | approval.execute, system.mutation, raw_data_export |
| gitlab.release_notes.prepare | read_only | read_only | content.draft, data.aggregate | external_dispatch, system.mutation, raw_data_export |

### Input contracts

| capability_id | input | type | required | semantic_type | entity_reference |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| gitlab.project.search_context | namespace | string | yes | project_namespace | yes |
| gitlab.project.search_context | project | string | yes | project_name | yes |
| gitlab.project.search_context | query | string | yes |  | no |
| gitlab.project.search_context | limit | integer | no |  | no |
| gitlab.project.search_context | backend_options | object | no | backend_options | no |
| gitlab.issue.prepare | namespace | string | yes | project_namespace | yes |
| gitlab.issue.prepare | project | string | yes | project_name | yes |
| gitlab.issue.prepare | title | string | yes |  | no |
| gitlab.issue.prepare | body | string | yes |  | no |
| gitlab.issue.prepare | labels | array<string> | no |  | no |
| gitlab.issue.prepare | assignees | array<string> | no |  | no |
| gitlab.issue.prepare | backend_options | object | no | backend_options | no |
| gitlab.mr.comment.prepare | namespace | string | yes | project_namespace | yes |
| gitlab.mr.comment.prepare | project | string | yes | project_name | yes |
| gitlab.mr.comment.prepare | merge_request_iid | integer | yes | merge_request_ref | yes |
| gitlab.mr.comment.prepare | comment_purpose | string | yes |  | no |
| gitlab.mr.comment.prepare | context | string | yes |  | no |
| gitlab.mr.comment.prepare | backend_options | object | no | backend_options | no |
| gitlab.pipeline.trigger.request | namespace | string | yes | project_namespace | yes |
| gitlab.pipeline.trigger.request | project | string | yes | project_name | yes |
| gitlab.pipeline.trigger.request | pipeline_ref | string | yes | pipeline_ref | yes |
| gitlab.pipeline.trigger.request | ref | string | yes |  | no |
| gitlab.pipeline.trigger.request | inputs | object | no |  | no |
| gitlab.pipeline.trigger.request | backend_options | object | no | backend_options | no |
| gitlab.release_notes.prepare | namespace | string | yes | project_namespace | yes |
| gitlab.release_notes.prepare | project | string | yes | project_name | yes |
| gitlab.release_notes.prepare | range | string | yes |  | no |
| gitlab.release_notes.prepare | audience | string | no |  | no |
| gitlab.release_notes.prepare | backend_options | object | no | backend_options | no |

| gitlab.mr.comment.prepare | gitlab-fronting | write | write_adjacent | 6 | gitlab.api.prepare_merge_request_comment |
